hypothesis: Incorporating ternary metal sublattices (two distinct metal species at s-d border positions) into the chemical template framework will yield stable superhydrides with non-integer stoichiometries that exhibit higher superconducting transition temperatures than their binary analogues, because the electronic mismatch between the two metal species will induce additional charge localization at interstitial quasi-atom sites, strengthening the template effect and producing more complex three-dimensional hydrogen clathrate cages with enhanced electron-phonon coupling constants exceeding those observed in the best binary superhydrides identified in this study.
